title: Elementary construction occupations n.e.c.
description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of security workers and security guards. All protective service supervisors not listed separately above. Patrol assigned area to prevent fish and game law violations. Investigate reports of damage to crops or property by wildlife. Compile biological data. (Based on 66 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Examine logs or lumber to plan the best cuts and weigh options based on blueprints or work orders.
Position and clamp stock on tables, conveyors, or carriages, and adjust bolts, clamps, or table heights as needed.
Trim lumber to straighten rough edges or remove defects, using circular saws, and guide workpieces during cutting.
Measure and mark stock for cuts, and inspect workpieces to verify cut accuracy and stock quality.
Set up, operate, or tend various saws or machines that cut or trim wood to specified dimensions, and monitor their operation.
Unload materials and tools from work trucks and organize materials for construction use, ensuring site safety.
Erect scaffolding and set ladders to facilitate access for construction and roofing tasks.
Clean work areas and remove debris after construction tasks, maintaining a safe job site.
Dig trenches, backfill excavations, and compact earth to grade specifications using appropriate manual and powered tools.
Install insulation and sound-absorbing materials, measure and cut for fitting around obstructions as needed.
Control traffic around work zones and ensure safety protocols are followed while working near roadways.
Provide assistance to skilled trades like carpenters or masons, and perform support duties related to construction work.
Trace out short circuits in wiring, using a test meter.
Cut pipe, disassemble and remove damaged or worn pipe, and transport materials to installation sites.
Clean work areas, machines, and equipment, using solvents, water, or chemical solutions.
Use fillers, adhesives, or grouting materials to correct surface imperfections or fill chipped, cracked, or broken tiles.
Apply caulk, sealants, or other agents to installed surfaces and apply grout between joints of bricks or tiles.
Arrange or store materials, tools, machines, or equipment as well as maintain tools and equipment.
Move or position materials such as marble slabs, and apply shingles or asphalt to roofing materials.
Perform ventilation tasks and check to ensure that completed roofs are watertight.
Repair and replace fixtures, leaking or broken pipes, and assist in the preparation or installation of tile, brick, or stone surfaces.
Cut timbers, drill holes in lumber, and modify material mixing or cleaning procedures according to installation requirements.
Erect scaffolding and install gas burners to convert furnaces from other fuel types.
Remove excess grout from brick or tile joints using sponges or trowels; remove or limit contamination following hazardous substance emergencies.
Clean and maintain tools and equipment, as well as order new parts to maintain inventory.
Install and seal air ducts and apply weather-stripping and insulation materials to improve energy efficiency.
Perform routine maintenance on machines including inspecting motors, drives, and belts, and apply protective materials to prevent defects.
Discuss fencing needs with customers, estimate costs, and design or fabricate fence panels and gates.
Install various types of bolts and tighten anchored truss bolts using turnbuckles, ensuring structural integrity.
Prepare hazardous materials for removal or storage while complying with safety disposal procedures.
Inspect buildings to identify required weatherization measures, including repair work, modifications, or replacements.
Recommend weatherization techniques to clients according to their needs and applicable energy regulations and codes.
Prepare work stations for mechanics and repairers, and hold or supply tools, parts, equipment, and supplies for other workers.
Position vehicles, machinery, and equipment for assembly or installation using hand tools and power tools.
Assemble and maintain physical structures using hand or power tools, and paint or repair roofs, windows, and doors of buildings.
Disassemble broken or defective equipment for repair and reassemble once repairs are complete.
Transfer tools, parts, and supplies to and from work stations and clean or lubricate machinery and tools as required.
Inspect, operate, or test machinery to diagnose malfunctions and ensure proper functioning, and tend to equipment for safe operation.
Install, repair, and replace equipment, fixtures, and systems using hand tools or power tools, and adjust wiring and plumbing as needed.
Plan and lay out repair work using diagrams and blueprints while recording type and cost of maintenance or repair work.
Set up, operate, and maintain drilling rigs and machines for excavations, ensuring safety procedures are followed during operations.
Drive trucks or transport equipment to and from work sites, ensuring materials are loaded and moved according to specifications.
Measure and verify levels of excavated materials to prevent equipment overloads, and monitor loading processes for compliance.
Gather, test, and monitor geological data or drilling operations to determine appropriate drilling patterns and techniques.
Perform routine maintenance and repairs on drilling and blasting equipment, including cleaning and lubricating machines.
Document system operations, performance, and maintenance for photovoltaic (PV) systems, and ensure conformity to performance expectations.
Plan installation procedures for photovoltaic (PV) systems based on customer needs and site conditions, ensuring compliance with regulations.
Install and maintain electrical connections and wiring for photovoltaic (PV) systems, ensuring safety and adherence to standards.
Signal workers for needed movements or equipment adjustments to ensure safety and efficiency at work sites.
Direct workers engaged in placing blocks or outriggers to prevent capsizing of machines when lifting heavy loads.
Set up or inspect equipment prior to operation and ensure they are functioning properly.
Move materials over short distances, such as around a construction site, factory, or warehouse, and position items for loading or unloading.
Monitor compliance with standard operating procedures for loss prevention, physical security, or risk management, and report safety concerns.
Inspect buildings, equipment, or access points to determine security risks and address safety concerns.
Write warnings and citations for illegally parked vehicles, and investigate complaints regarding contested parking citations.
Conduct investigations of damage to crops, property, or habitat, and provide financial loss estimates or compensation recommendations.
Train establishment personnel in loss prevention activities and supervise the activities of seasonal workers.
Operate machinery to perform activities such as backfilling excavations or breaking rock and inspect equipment before operations.
Respond to critical incidents, such as catastrophic events, and coordinate with departments to assist in investigations or training.
Observe activities in assigned areas to detect hazards, disturbances, or safety infractions.
Inspect recreational facilities and equipment for cleanliness and safety hazards and damage or wear.
Provide warnings to recreational participants about inclement weather, unsafe areas, or illegal conduct.
Direct traffic movement and warn of hazards using signs, flags, lanterns, and hand signals.
Patrol the premises to prevent or detect intrusion and protect property.
Report unsafe behavior and communicate rules to students and adults.
Train security personnel on protective procedures, first aid, and fire safety.
Drive vehicles to transport crews and equipment to work sites.
Inspect and adjust security equipment to ensure operational status.
Investigate disturbances or suspicious activities on the premises and report accordingly.
Record production and operational data, such as the amount of materials processed and personnel information on specified forms or reports.
Prepare and maintain logs, reports, or other documentation of work performed and employee activities.
Inspect equipment, work progress, construction sites, or job sites to verify safety and ensure specifications are met.
Monitor and adjust operations of geothermal power plant equipment or systems, and assess system performance at various levels.
Operate machinery used in the production process or assist machine operators; load and unload items from machines and conveyors.
Train workers in construction methods, safety procedures, or equipment operation and supervise installation projects for compliance with safety standards.
Provide technical assistance in areas such as solar electric systems, coordinate or schedule installations, and monitor work of contractors.
Identify and correct malfunctions of geothermal plant or other equipment, order or requisition materials, and arrange for repairs.
Supervise, coordinate, or schedule the activities of construction or extractive workers, assigning work based on material or personnel requirements.
Clean and maintain machinery, equipment, and work areas to ensure proper functioning and safe working conditions.
Estimate material, time, and staffing requirements for projects based on work orders, specifications, and experience.
Direct workers in transportation or related services, such as pumping, moving, storing, or loading/unloading materials.
Schedule the use of recreation facilities, such as golf courses, tennis courts, bowling alleys, or softball diamonds.
Sort materials, such as metals, glass, wood, paper, or plastics, into appropriate containers for recycling.
Collect recyclable materials from curbside for delivery to designated facilities.
Maintain equipment storage areas to ensure that inventory is protected.
Operate forklifts, pallet jacks, power lifts, or front-end loaders to load bales or other heavy items onto trucks for shipping.
Inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established standards in areas such as cleanliness or maintenance.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials.
Prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities, operations, production, or work-related accidents.
Clean, inspect, or lubricate recyclable collection equipment or perform routine maintenance on recycling equipment.
